mission.title: grpc: Undefined symbol `_ruby_abi_version` regression with Xcode 13 -> 14
mission.description: Undefined symbol `_ruby_abi_version` regression with Xcode 13 -> 14. This issue affects 30+ users.

steps.title: Identify grpc troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your grpc setup:
gRPC library — check your language-specific package (e.g., pip show grpcio, npm ls @grpc/grpc-js)
Look for error: `linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)`

steps.title: Review grpc configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant grpc configuration:
### What version of gRPC and what language are you using?
grpc: 1.48.0
language: ruby 2.7.6

### What operating system (Linux, Windows,...) and version?
MacOS 12.6
Apple M1 chip

### What runtime / compiler are you using (e.g.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Undefined symbol `_ruby_abi_version` regression with Xcode…
steps.description:
The next Ruby version, 3.2, will have builtin ABI checking (see ruby/ruby#5474). This requires that the symbol `ruby_abi_version` is present in the shared object, otherwise the object fails to load.

For example, this is a small repro in Ruby 3.2:

```
$ ruby -Isrc/ruby/lib -e "require
```yaml
Undefined symbols for architecture arm64:
  "_ruby_abi_version", referenced from:
     -exported_symbol[s_list] command line option
ld: symbol(s) not found for architecture arm64
clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
make: *** [grpc_c.bundle] Error 1
```

steps.title: Upgrade grpc to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade grpc:
Update the grpc dependency in your project manifest to the latest version.
See the project's releases: https://github.com/grpc/grpc/releases

steps.title: Confirm Undefined symbol `_ruby_abi_version` regression… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test grpc to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that `linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)` no longer appears.

resolution.summary: The next Ruby version, 3.2, will have builtin ABI checking (see ruby/ruby#5474). This requires that the symbol `ruby_abi_version` is present in the shared object, otherwise the object fails to load.
